question.text: An index fund must invest at least what percentage of its assets in the securities of the index it tracks?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per SEBI regulations, an index fund must invest a minimum of 95% of its total assets in the securities of the index it is tracking (like Nifty 50 or Sensex). The remaining 5% can be kept as cash or in money market instruments for liquidity. **The Trap:** 100% sounds logical but is impractical — the fund needs some cash to handle day-to-day redemptions. 90% and 80% are too low. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under passive fund management. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Index funds are easy to explain to clients — "your money follows the Nifty/Sensex." Low cost, no fund manager bias. Perfect for clients who want simplicity.
